name: S&P 500
summary: Day 73-74 (Mon-Tue). **US DECOUPLING ESSENTIALLY HOLDING DESPITE WAR ESCALATION: S&P 500 7,412.84 fresh record close +0.2% 1D / +8.7% 1M / +8.5% 3M; NASDAQ 26,274.13 +0.1% 1D / +14.7% 1M / +16.3% 3M; Dow 49,704.47 +0.2% 1D. SMH $576.31 +1.7% 1D / +31.9% 1M / +41.9% 3M (AI-CapEx demand exceeding helium-constraint impact). VIX +6.9% 1D to 18.38 — first warning US complacency cracking but still NOT panic (cycle low ~13-14 late April; March crisis-peak 32+).** US 10Y yield 4.41% (+1.1% 1D, +2.2% 1M, +5.7% 3M) — oil-driven inflation expectations keeping real yields competitive vs zero-coupon gold; 2Y 3.60%; DXY 98.13 (+0.2% 1D). **Drivers of VIX +6.9%:** Trump 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE' rejection + WH Sit-Room meeting May 11 weighing renewed strikes + Hormuz transit deteriorated to 2-5 ships/day per Aramco + Agios Fanourios VLCC U-turn + Houthi-attribution-unconfirmed strike near Hodeidah possibly breaking Day 80 quiet. **Sectoral repricing today:** XLE +2.6% 1D (Brent up + Exxon +3.5% — sector breadth strongest in 5 sessions); CF Industries +8.2% 1D (THE MOVE OF THE DAY — Modi's fertilizer-50% appeal + UBS PT $97→$140); Nutrien +4.3%; BASF +3.5% (fertilizer chain); Linde +2.3% (industrial-gas pricing power); Uranium (URA) +3.7% 1D / +12.3% 1M (Iran HEU concession + return clause = nuclear file now central diplomatic axis); Exxon +3.5% 1D (Brent + Lavan offline); Lockheed +1.1% 1D / -16.5% 1M (defense-rotation paradox: Q1 miss April 23 EPS $6.44 vs $7.28; FCF -$291M; supply-chain delays + fixed-cost-program margin compression; ITA -1.6% 1M); Frontline -3.4% 1D / +11.1% 1M (Evercore ISI downgrade citing 2027-28 oversupply); Cheniere $240.70 +0.5% 1D / -9.1% 1M. **Wheat +3.2%, Corn +3.2% (fertilizer cost pass-through); RBOB Gasoline -3.7% 1D (counter-trend mean-reversion after +80% 3M).** **US-vs-India decoupling is STRUCTURAL story of the day:** US +0.2% record vs Indian Sensex -1.7% May 11. Aluminum LME -2.3% 1D = COUNTER-NARRATIVE (cancelled-warrant decline -7% to 26,500t signals reduced near-term physical demand; China property + global industrial moderation; not all crisis-linked moves are crisis-driven). **Watch level: VIX >20 confirms regime shift.** Until then, rotation signal not regime change.

recent_signals.headline: US DECOUPLING ESSENTIALLY HOLDING: S&P 500 7,412.84 fresh record +0.2% / NASDAQ 26,274.13 +0.1% / Dow 49,704.47 +0.2% — first warning VIX +6.9% to 18.38 (cycle low ~13-14; March crisis-peak 32+; still NOT panic). US 10Y yield 4.41% +1.1% 1D (oil-driven inflation expectations). DRIVERS: Trump 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE' rejection + WH Sit-Room weighing renewed strikes + Hormuz 2-5 ships/day per Aramco + Agios Fanourios VLCC U-turn + Houthi-attribution-unconfirmed strike Hodeidah breaking Day 80 quiet. **SECTORAL — CF Industries +8.2% 1D = MOVE OF THE DAY (Modi fertilizer-50% appeal + UBS PT $97→$140; YTD +71%); Nutrien +4.3%, BASF +3.5%, Linde +2.3% (fertilizer chain regime shift); URA +3.7% 1D / +12.3% 1M (Iran HEU concession = nuclear file now central diplomatic axis); Exxon +3.5% (Brent + Lavan offline); XLE +2.6% sector breadth strongest in 5 sessions; LMT +1.1% 1D / -16.5% 1M (defense-rotation paradox: Q1 EPS $6.44 vs $7.28 + FCF -$291M + supply-chain delays); Frontline -3.4% 1D / +11.1% 1M (Evercore ISI downgrade 2027-28 oversupply); Wheat +3.2%, Corn +3.2% (fertilizer cost pass-through); Aluminum -2.3% (COUNTER-NARRATIVE — cancelled-warrant decline -7% to 26,500t signals reduced near-term physical demand, China property + global industrial moderation; not all crisis-linked moves are crisis-driven); RBOB Gasoline -3.7% 1D (counter-trend mean-reversion after +80% 3M).** US-vs-India decoupling structural story (S&P +0.2% record vs Sensex -1.7% May 11; INR ₹95.54 record). Goldman raised 2026 Brent average $77→$85; JPM stays $60 — largest bulge-bracket divergence of cycle. **Watch level: VIX >20 confirms regime shift; until then, rotation signal not regime change.**

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY — MAY 8 CLOSES OPERATIVE: S&P 500 7,398.93 RECORD (+0.84% 1D, +8.4% 1M); NASDAQ 26,247.08 (+1.7% 1D, +15.0% 1M); SMH $566.54 (+4.9% 1D, +31.7% 1M); KOSPI 7,498.00 (+36.5% 1M, +41.5% 3M); VIX 17.19 cycle-low. **Two days of unsettled news flow accumulated against frozen prices** — Sunday bulk-carrier-near-Doha strike (NEW, first commercial vessel attack since CMA CGM May 5, FIRST inside Qatari EEZ), CENTCOM precision tally 58/4 (UPDATED), Qatar PM Miami trip + Axios Qatar-IRGC scoop (UPDATED-MAJOR), Bahrain 41 IRGC detentions (NEW), Kharg satellite slick (NEW), UAE air-defense scale refined (UPDATED). **Monday May 11 Asian open will be first re-pricing event.** Markets pricing Branch 3 (de-escalation) for risk assets, Branch 2 (trinary regime) for energy, Branch 1 (kinetic) for nobody — most internally inconsistent positioning of cycle. Defense complex actively unwinding (LMT $506.51 -1.2% 1D, -18.8% 1M, -20.2% 3M); Cheniere -2.7% 1D on $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM; CF Industries -3.1% on Q1 EPS blowout (sell-the-news). **VIX 17.19 cycle-low is the most aggressive 'war doesn't matter for equities' trade since 2022 Russia-Ukraine post-shock period — if Iran rejects past May 21, VIX could spike to 28-32 in 2-3 sessions. Asymmetric long-vol setup of cycle.** **KOSPI +36.5% MoM is THE most reversible position in world if helium buffers deplete past mid-June (~5 weeks remaining).**
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED multi-source price tape (May 8 close); accumulated unpriced news = CONFIRMED ACTION (UKMTO/CENTCOM/Axios/Bahrain MoI/Copernicus/UAE Defense Ministry primaries)

recent_signals.headline: MAY 8 TAPE: TWO SIMULTANEOUS REGIMES COEXISTING. S&P 500 NEW RECORD 7,398.93 (+0.84% 1D, +8.4% 1M); NASDAQ COMPOSITE 26,247.08 (+1.7% 1D, +15.0% 1M); SMH $566.54 (+4.9% 1D, +31.7% 1M, +39.3% 3M — Micron, Sandisk +15%; Nvidia +2%); KOSPI 7,498.00 (+0.1% 1D, +36.5% 1M, +41.5% 3M = LARGEST 30-DAY MOVE IN ANY MAJOR INDEX IN RECENT MEMORY); MSCI EM ETF $67.94 (+2.0% 1D, +12.7% 1M); VIX 17.08-17.19 cycle-low. **Crisis-decoupled assets running AI super-cycle independently** (markets pricing-through helium tail-risk because buffers haven't drawn down enough to bite Q2). **Crisis-driven assets continue to price trinary regime:** Brent $101.73 (+1.66% 1D, range $96-104 = trinary signature); Heating Oil +2.2% 1D / +61.3% 3M; RBOB Gasoline +2.0% 1D / +77.6% 3M; Frontline +3.6% 1D / +13.5% 1M; BOAT +2.3% 1D. **Defense complex unwinding**: LMT $506.51 (-1.2% 1D, -18.8% 1M, -20.2% 3M) — cleanest 'ceasefire takes the war premium out' signal in cycle, despite May 7 IRGC destroyer attacks + May 8 second tanker disabling + CENTCOM 70-tanker blockade scale + Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' Hormuz speech. Markets choosing diplomatic-track narrative over trinary-regime operational reality. **Cheniere -2.7% 1D, -9.7% 1M** = $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM (long-term LNG hedged at pre-crisis prices); CCO 'astounded that global gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' **CF Industries -3.1% 1D** on Q1 EPS $3.98 vs $2.50 est blowout — sell-the-news after +54% YTD run. Bonds: US 10Y 4.36% (-0.6% 1D, +1.7% 1M); 2Y 3.60%; DXY 97.84 (-0.4% 1D, -1.0% 1M). May 8 jobs print stronger than expected — reduces immediate June-cut probability. **KOSPI +36.5% MoM is THE most reversible position in the world if helium buffers deplete past mid-June.**
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED multi-source price tape; two-regime structural read CONFIRMED via cross-asset disambiguation (defense decay + Cheniere derivative blowup vs SMH/KOSPI breakout); helium tail-risk pricing-through CONFIRMED via Cheniere CCO public comment
